close_reason: Fixed in commit 28e4703 (add Debug impls across all backend structs)
description:
src/lib.rs:106-112 'pub struct GcpSmBackend { ... }' has no Debug impl. The struct's containing Arc<dyn SecretStore> is constructed in inventory factories; if a caller's diagnostic uses '{:?}' on an Arc holding GcpSmBackend, they get... well, Arc<dyn SecretStore> doesn't implement Debug either since SecretStore doesn't require it.

Direct impact is small (callers cannot Debug-print the trait object), but a Debug impl on the concrete type is useful when:
  - Writing tests that print the backend
  - Library consumers wrap it in their own Debug-deriving struct (forces them to write a manual impl too)
  - Future diagnostic tooling

Because the struct holds a Zeroizing<String> token, Debug must NOT print the token. The right pattern is:
  impl std::fmt::Debug for GcpSmBackend {
      fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
          f.debug_struct("GcpSmBackend")
              .field("project", &self.project)
              .field("secret", &self.secret)
              .field("version", &self.version)
              .field("access_token", &"<redacted>")
              .finish()
      }
  }

SecretVersion (currently has no derive) would also need #[derive(Debug)] or a manual impl.

Engineering basis: idiomatic Rust: 'Debug on almost everything' (per /review-rusty type-design rules). Skipping Debug for security reasons is right; SKIPPING because no one wrote it is wrong. Write a redacted Debug impl.
title: GcpSmBackend has no Debug impl; diagnostic blindness on Arc<dyn> errors
